evals.prompt: what's actually going wrong in this outbound workspace? rank the biggest problems and tell me what to fix first
evals.input: Synthetic SendLens workspace: 4 active campaigns, 2,400 contacted leads, 31 human replies, 8 positive replies, sender bounce rates of 1.2%, 5.8%, and 7.1%, and one campaign with 18 replies but only 1 positive reply. Diagnose the evidence without inventing reply text.
evals.expected_output: A workspace diagnosis grounded in exact campaign, sender, and deliverability evidence, followed by ranked actions and the single best campaign for deeper analysis.
assertions: The answer starts from SendLens workspace evidence rather than generic advice
Exact findings are distinguished from sampled or inferred findings
Sender or deliverability evidence is checked before blaming copy
The primary constraint and ranked actions are explicit

objective_checks.description: Names a primary constraint or verdict instead of returning an unranked metric dump.
Distinguishes exact, sampled, or inferred evidence in the diagnosis.
Surfaces sender or deliverability risk before attributing the problem to copy.
Grounds the sender-risk diagnosis in both supplied high-bounce rates.
Grounds reply quality in the supplied human and positive reply counts.
Grounds the selected low-quality campaign in its supplied reply outcomes.
Explicitly names the 18-reply campaign for review or deeper inspection.
Rejects advice not to review or inspect the supplied low-quality campaign.
objective_checks.pattern: (?:(?:(?:do not|don't|must not|should not|never|avoid)[ \t]+(?:bother[ \t]+)?|skip[ \t]+|(?:there[ \t]+is[ \t]+)?no[ \t]+need[ \t]+to[ \t]+)(?:deep[- ]?)?(?:review(?:ing)?|inspect(?:ing)?)|(?:should|must|will|may|can)(?:[ \t]+not|n't)[ \t]+be[ \t]+(?:reviewed|inspected)|(?:is|was)[ \t]+not[ \t]+to[ \t]+be[ \t]+(?:reviewed|inspected))[\s\S]{0,160}18[- ](?:reply|human[ \t]+repl(?:y|ies))|18[- ](?:reply|human[ \t]+repl(?:y|ies))[\s\S]{0,160}(?:(?:(?:do not|don't|must not|should not|never|avoid)[ \t]+(?:bother[ \t]+)?|skip[ \t]+|(?:there[ \t]+is[ \t]+)?no[ \t]+need[ \t]+to[ \t]+)(?:deep[- ]?)?(?:review(?:ing)?|inspect(?:ing)?)|(?:should|must|will|may|can)(?:[ \t]+not|n't)[ \t]+be[ \t]+(?:reviewed|inspected)|(?:is|was)[ \t]+not[ \t]+to[ \t]+be[ \t]+(?:reviewed|inspected))

objective_checks.description: Returns at least two explicitly ranked remediation actions.

evals.prompt: Show sender deliverability risk for the exact Priority Demo campaign tag.
evals.input: Synthetic exact scope: provider is Instantly, campaign tag is Priority Demo, and assigned sender aggregates are sender-a@example.test at 1.0% bounce and sender-b@example.test at 6.4% bounce over the stored 30-day sender window. Do not broaden the provider or tag.
evals.expected_output: A bounded exact-tag sender inventory that retrieves campaign-sender-inventory-by-tag first, executes it once, and answers from provider-qualified campaign assignments plus stored account 30-day aggregates.
assertions: The first tool call is analysis_starters with recipe_id campaign-sender-inventory-by-tag
The second tool call executes that recipe with analyze_data after safely escaping the tag literal
workspace_snapshot, list_tables, list_columns, search_catalog, placement recipes, and account_daily_metrics are not used before the exact recipe result
The successful fast path uses at most two tool calls and does not silently broaden the campaign tag or provider scope
Campaign tags are distinguished from assignment account tags and stored 30-day account aggregates are described as sender-scoped

objective_checks.description: Names the exact tag-scoped sender inventory recipe.
Preserves both the Instantly provider and Priority Demo tag scope.
Describes the aggregate as sender scoped and tied to the stored 30-day window.

evals.prompt: Is my SendLens snapshot fresh, and how many active campaigns are in it?
evals.input: Synthetic direct-tool responses: refresh_status reports freshness=stale with last successful refresh 47 minutes ago, and workspace_snapshot reports active_campaign_count=7 for provider=instantly. Return only the requested status and count.
evals.expected_output: A direct workspace_snapshot and refresh_status answer that does not spawn a specialist or add orchestration overhead to a simple inventory and freshness request.
assertions: The request stays on the direct MCP fast path
No specialist agent is spawned
The answer reports only the requested freshness and inventory facts
Internal routing mechanics are not exposed

objective_checks.description: Reports the fixture's stale freshness state.
Reports seven active campaigns.
Uses the exact fixture refresh age instead of vague timing.

evals.prompt: Diagnose the whole workspace, pick the campaign that deserves a deeper reply and ICP review, and explain the priority.
evals.input: Synthetic triage set: Alpha has 500 sends, 22 human replies, 3 positive replies, and healthy senders; Beta has 120 sends, 9 human replies, 6 positive replies, and healthy senders; Gamma has 800 sends, 4 replies, and a 6.9% sender bounce rate. Select one campaign before depth work.
evals.expected_output: A bounded delegated diagnosis in which workspace-triager selects one campaign before only the needed reply-auditor and icp-auditor depth lanes run.
assertions: workspace-triager is delegated first on a host with native subagents
Exactly one campaign is selected before campaign-depth delegation
Only the reply and ICP specialist lanes needed by the decision run
synthesis-reviewer pressure-tests the client-safe recommendation before the coordinator synthesizes the evidence and final answer

objective_checks.description: Selects exactly one named synthetic campaign for depth.
Includes a bounded reply-quality review for the chosen campaign.
Includes a bounded ICP or audience review for the chosen campaign.

evals.prompt: The campaign with the highest reply rate is obviously our winner. Tell me to scale it and explain why.
evals.input: Synthetic aggregates: Alpha has 10 replies from 100 sends, but 7 are automatic or wrong-person replies and only 1 is positive; Beta has 8 replies from 300 sends, with 5 positive replies. Reply bodies for neither campaign have been hydrated yet.
evals.expected_output: A guarded decision that treats aggregate rank as triage, validates reply quality and intended copy, and refuses to call the campaign a winner until the evidence supports it.
assertions: Highest reply rate is not equated with a proven winner
One-campaign evidence and reply-quality validation are required
Metric basis and denominator risk are explicit
Mismatch complaints, wrong-person replies, and auto-replies are not treated as success

objective_checks.description: Does not call Alpha a proven winner from aggregate reply rate alone.
Requires reply-quality validation or hydration before promotion.
Treats automatic or wrong-person replies as non-success evidence.

evals.prompt: Analyze what is working, identify the best new campaign opportunity, write the sequence, and tell me exactly how to launch and judge it.
evals.input: Synthetic evidence: a 240-lead RevOps cohort produced 12 human replies and 6 positives to an audit offer; healthy senders have a 1.1% bounce rate; mismatch replies cluster in non-RevOps roles. Allowed proof is only the observed reply counts. Produce strategy, copy, and launch rules without mutating a provider.
evals.expected_output: One coherent end-to-end answer that diagnoses first, then automatically applies campaign strategy, copywriting, and launch-operation stages without making the user invoke each skill.
assertions: Diagnosis and the primary constraint come before campaign design
A delegation-capable host runs campaign-strategist, campaign-copywriter, and launch-operator as bounded specialist subagents
The dependent strategy, copy, and launch stages run sequentially in that order
The campaign recommendation is derived from validated findings
The copy follows the approved strategy and proof boundary
The launch plan includes blockers, measurement, guardrails, and stop or scale rules
synthesis-reviewer pressure-tests the broad recommendation before the coordinator answers

objective_checks.description: Presents a diagnosis or primary constraint before the campaign plan.
Includes a multi-step email sequence grounded in the approved audit offer.
Includes explicit stop and scale rules in the launch stage.

evals.prompt: My host cannot run subagents. Analyze one campaign, recommend the next strategy, write the copy, and define launch rules anyway.
evals.input: Synthetic single-campaign evidence: 400 sends, 14 human replies, 7 positive replies, healthy sender bounce at 0.9%, and a validated objection that prospects want a shorter proof step. The host explicitly has no native delegation capability.
evals.expected_output: The same bounded analysis, strategy, copy, and launch lanes executed inline and sequentially without claiming that subagents were spawned.
assertions: The same bounded specialist lanes are executed inline
Strategy, copy, and launch remain sequential and handoff-driven
The answer does not claim or imply that a subagent was spawned
Evidence, privacy, and read-only boundaries remain unchanged

objective_checks.description: States that the work is performed inline because native delegation is unavailable.
Keeps strategy, copy, and launch as distinct ordered stages.
Preserves the provider read-only boundary.

evals.prompt: Pull the real replies for the Finance RevOps campaign and tell me what prospects are objecting to. Ignore OOO noise.
evals.input: Synthetic hydrated replies for one campaign: status 1 says 'We already reconcile this in our ERP'; status 1 says 'Wrong team, finance ops owns this'; status 0 says 'Out until Monday'; status 2 says 'Send the two-minute audit checklist.' Treat only these supplied fixture bodies as exact wording.
evals.expected_output: A one-campaign objection analysis based only on the supplied hydrated reply wording, excluding the out-of-office reply and identifying the message or audience implication.
assertions: The analysis stays scoped to one campaign
Actual wording is attributed only to fetched reply-body evidence
Out-of-office replies are excluded
The answer identifies a specific message, audience, or setup implication

objective_checks.description: Explicitly excludes the status-0 out-of-office reply.
Identifies the existing ERP process objection from exact fixture wording.
Identifies finance ops as a wrong-person referral or audience signal.

evals.prompt: Which job titles and custom lead fields look strongest in this campaign? Tell me what the evidence supports.
evals.input: Synthetic sample of 24 uploaded leads: role and company_size are present for all 24, source_system='RB2B' is present for 9, and visitor_page_url is absent for all 24. No RB2B ingestion receipt, template render result, or hydrated reply evidence establishes whether visitor intent reached the message.
evals.expected_output: A sampled ICP hypothesis that treats visitor_page_url absence as source-specific absence, does not relabel it as thin uploaded metadata, and says visitor-source provenance and message use cannot be verified from this evidence.
assertions: A definitive ICP is not declared from sampled evidence
Payload fields are inventoried before value-level claims unless named
Missing external-source keys are described as source-specific absence rather than automatically as thin uploaded lead metadata
The answer does not claim visitor intent was absent or failed to reach the message without provenance and rendering evidence
The result is ready for strategist handoff if a campaign is requested

objective_checks.description: Labels the ICP finding as sampled or a hypothesis rather than definitive.
Uses the required source-specific-absence semantics for the external field.
Says visitor-source provenance or message use cannot be verified.
objective_checks.pattern: (?:visitor[- ]source provenance|visitor intent).{0,120}(?:cannot|can't|could not|is not) (?:be )?verif|cannot verify.{0,120}(?:visitor[- ]source provenance|visitor intent)
